title: नाम रामायणम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री नाम रामायणम् ॥

॥ बालकाण्डम् ॥
शुद्धब्रह्मपरात्पर राम।
कालात्मकपरमेश्वर राम।
शेषतल्पसुखनिद्रित राम।
ब्रह्माद्यमरप्रार्थित राम।
चण्डकिरणकुलमण्डन राम।
श्रीमद्दशरथनन्दन राम।
कौसल्यासुखवर्धन राम।
विश्वामित्रप्रियधन राम।
घोरताटकाघातक राम।
मारीचादिनिपातक राम।
कौशिकमखसंरक्षक राम।
श्रीमदहल्योद्धारक राम।
गौतममुनिसम्पूजित राम।
सुरमुनिवरगणसंस्तुत राम।
नाविकधावितमृदुपद राम।
मिथिलापुरजनमोहक राम।
विदेहमानसरंजक राम।
त्र्यम्बककार्मुकभञ्जक राम।
सीतार्पितवरमालिक राम।
कृतवैवाहिककौतुक राम।
भार्गवदर्पविनाशक राम।
श्रीमदयोध्यापालक राम॥

॥ अयोध्याकाण्डम् ॥
अगणितगुणगणभूषित राम।
अवनीतनयाकामित राम।
राकाचन्द्रसमानन राम।
पितृवाक्यश्रितकानन राम।
प्रियगुहविनिवेदितपद राम।
तत्क्षालितनिजमृदुपद राम।
भरद्वाजमुखानन्दक राम।
चित्रकूटाद्रिनिकेतन राम।
दशरथसन्ततचिन्तित राम।
कैकेयीतनयार्थित राम।
विरचितनिजपितृकर्मक राम।
भरतार्पितनिजपादुक राम॥

॥ अरण्यकाण्डम् ॥
दण्डकावनजनपावन राम।
दुष्टविराधविनाशन राम।
शरभंगसुतीक्ष्णार्चित राम।
अगस्त्यानुग्रहवर्धित राम।
गृध्राधिपसंसेवित राम।
पंचवटीतटसुस्थित राम।
शूर्पणखार्तिविधायक राम।
खरदूषणमुखसूदक राम।
सीताप्रियहरिणानुग राम।
मारीचार्तिकृताशुग राम।
विनष्टसीतान्वेषक राम।
गृध्राधिपगतिदायक राम।
शबरीदत्तफलाशन राम।
कबन्धबाहुच्छेदक राम॥

॥ किष्किन्धाकाण्डम् ॥
हनुमत्सेवितनिजपद राम।
नतसुग्रीवाभीष्टद राम।
गर्वितवालिसंहारक राम।
वानरदूतप्रेषक राम।
हितकरलक्ष्मणसंयुत राम॥

॥ सुन्दरकाण्डम् ॥
कपिवरसन्ततसंस्मृत राम।
तद्गतिविघ्नध्वंसक राम।
सीताप्राणाधारक राम।
दुष्टदशाननदूषित राम।
शिष्टहनूमद्भूषित राम।
सीतावेदितकाकावन राम।
कृतचूडामणिदर्शन राम।
कपिवरवचनाश्वासित राम॥

॥ युद्धकाण्डम् ॥
रावणनिधनप्रस्थित राम।
वानरसैन्यसमावृत राम।
शोषितशरदीशार्थित राम।
पत्तिसमुद्रनिर्यात राम।
कुम्भकर्णशिरच्छेदक राम।
राक्षससंघविमर्दक राम।
अहिमहिरावणचारक राम।
संहृतदशमुखराक्षस राम।
विधिभवमुखसुरसंस्तुत राम।
खस्थितदशरथवीक्षित राम।
सीतादर्शनमोदित राम।
अभिषिक्तविभीषणनत राम।
पुष्पकयानारोहण राम।
भरद्वाजभिनिषेवण राम।
भरतप्राणप्रियकर राम।
साकेतपुरीभूषण राम।
सकलस्वीयसमानत राम।
रत्नलसत्पीठास्थित राम।
पट्टाभिषेकालंकृत राम।
पार्थिवकुलसम्मानित राम।
विभीषणार्पितरंगक राम।
कीशकुलानुग्रहकर राम।
सकलजीवसंरक्षक राम।
समस्तलोकोद्धारक राम॥

॥ उत्तरकाण्डम् ॥
आगतमुनिगणसंस्तुत राम।
विश्रुतदशकण्ठोद्भव राम।
सीतालिंगननिर्वृत राम।
नीतिसुरक्षितजनपद राम।
विपिनत्यागितजनकज राम।
कारितलवणासुरवध राम।
स्वर्गतशम्बुकसंस्तुत राम।
स्वतनयकुशलवनन्दित राम।
अश्वमेधक्रतुदीक्षित राम।
कालावेदितसुरपद राम।
आयोध्यकजनमुक्तिद राम।
विधिमुखविबुधानन्दक राम।
तेजोमयनिजरूपक राम।
संसृतिबन्धविमोचक राम।
धर्मस्थापनतत्पर राम।
भक्तिपरायणमुक्तिद राम।
सर्वचराचरपालक राम।
सर्वभवामयवारक राम।
वैकुण्ठालयसंस्थित राम।
नित्यनन्दपदस्थित राम॥

राम राम जय राजाराम।
राम राम जय सीताराम॥

॥ इति श्री नाम रामायणम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Nama Ramayanam ||

|| Bala-Kandam ||
Shuddha-Brahma-paratpara Rama.
Kalatmaka-Parameshvara Rama.
Shesha-talpa-sukha-nidrita Rama.
Brahma-adya-amara-prarthita Rama.
Chanda-kirana-kula-mandana Rama.
Shrimad-Dasharatha-nandana Rama.
Kausalya-sukha-vardhana Rama.
Vishvamitra-priya-dhana Rama.
Ghora-Tataka-ghataka Rama.
Maricha-adi-nipataka Rama.
Kaushika-makha-samrakshaka Rama.
Shrimad-Ahalya-uddhaaraka Rama.
Gautama-muni-sampujita Rama.
Sura-muni-vara-gana-samstuta Rama.
Navika-dhavita-mridu-pada Rama.
Mithila-pura-jana-mohaka Rama.
Videha-manasa-ranjaka Rama.
Tryambaka-karmuka-bhanjaka Rama.
Sita-arpita-vara-malika Rama.
Krita-vaivahika-kautuka Rama.
Bhargava-darpa-vinashaka Rama.
Shrimad-Ayodhya-palaka Rama.

|| Ayodhya-Kandam ||
Aganita-guna-gana-bhushita Rama.
Avanee-tanaya-kamita Rama.
Raka-chandra-samanana Rama.
Pitri-vakya-shrita-kanana Rama.
Priya-Guha-vinivedita-pada Rama.
Tat-kshalita-nija-mridu-pada Rama.
Bharadvaja-mukha-anandaka Rama.
Chitrakuta-adri-niketana Rama.
Dasharatha-santata-chintita Rama.
Kaikeyi-tanaya-arthita Rama.
Virachita-nija-pitri-karmaka Rama.
Bharata-arpita-nija-paduka Rama.

|| Aranya-Kandam ||
Dandaka-vana-jana-pavana Rama.
Dushta-Viradha-vinashana Rama.
Sharabhanga-Sutikshna-archita Rama.
Agastya-anugraha-vardhita Rama.
Gridhra-adhipa-samsevita Rama.
Panchavati-tata-susthita Rama.
Shurpanakha-arti-vidhayaka Rama.
Khara-Dushana-mukha-sudaka Rama.
Sita-priya-harina-anuga Rama.
Maricha-arti-krita-ashuga Rama.
Vinashta-Sita-anveshaka Rama.
Gridhra-adhipa-gati-dayaka Rama.
Shabari-datta-phala-ashana Rama.
Kabandha-bahu-chchhedaka Rama.

|| Kishkindha-Kandam ||
Hanumat-sevita-nija-pada Rama.
Nata-Sugriva-abhishta-da Rama.
Garvita-Vali-samharaka Rama.
Vanara-duta-preshaka Rama.
Hitakara-Lakshmana-samyuta Rama.

|| Sundara-Kandam ||
Kapi-vara-santata-samsmrita Rama.
Tad-gati-vighna-dhvamsaka Rama.
Sita-prana-adharaka Rama.
Dushta-Dashanana-dushita Rama.
Shishta-Hanumad-bhushita Rama.
Sita-vedita-kaka-avana Rama.
Krita-chudamani-darshana Rama.
Kapi-vara-vachana-ashvasita Rama.

|| Yuddha-Kandam ||
Ravana-nidhana-prasthita Rama.
Vanara-sainya-samavrita Rama.
Shoshita-sharad-isha-arthita Rama.
Patti-samudra-niryata Rama.
Kumbhakarna-shirah-chhedaka Rama.
Rakshasa-sangha-vimardaka Rama.
Ahi-Mahi-Ravana-charaka Rama.
Samhrita-Dashamukha-Rakshasa Rama.
Vidhi-Bhava-mukha-sura-samstuta Rama.
Kha-sthita-Dasharatha-vikshita Rama.
Sita-darshana-modita Rama.
Abhishikta-Vibhishana-nata Rama.
Pushpaka-yana-arohana Rama.
Bharadvaja-abhi-nishevana Rama.
Bharata-prana-priyakara Rama.
Saketa-puri-bhushana Rama.
Sakala-sviya-samanata Rama.
Ratna-lasat-pitha-asthita Rama.
Pattabhisheka-alankrita Rama.
Parthiva-kula-sammanita Rama.
Vibhishana-arpita-rangaka Rama.
Kisha-kula-anugraha-kara Rama.
Sakala-jiva-samrakshaka Rama.
Samasta-loka-uddharaka Rama.

|| Uttara-Kandam ||
Agata-muni-gana-samstuta Rama.
Vishruta-Dasha-kantha-udbhava Rama.
Sita-alingana-nirvrita Rama.
Niti-surakshita-janapada Rama.
Vipina-tyagita-Janakaja Rama.
Karita-Lavana-asura-vadha Rama.
Svargata-Shambuka-samstuta Rama.
Sva-tanaya-Kusha-Lava-nandita Rama.
Ashvamedha-kratu-dikshita Rama.
Kala-avedita-sura-pada Rama.
Ayodhyaka-jana-muktida Rama.
Vidhi-mukha-vibudha-anandaka Rama.
Tejo-maya-nija-rupaka Rama.
Samsriti-bandha-vimochaka Rama.
Dharma-sthapana-tatpara Rama.
Bhakti-parayana-muktida Rama.
Sarva-chara-achara-palaka Rama.
Sarva-bhava-amaya-varaka Rama.
Vaikuntha-alaya-samsthita Rama.
Nitya-nanda-pada-sthita Rama.

Rama Rama Jaya Raja Rama.
Rama Rama Jaya Sita Rama.

|| Iti Shri Nama Ramayanam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
बालकाण्ड: शुद्ध ब्रह्म परात्पर राम, कालात्मक परमेश्वर राम, शेषतल्प पर सुखपूर्वक शयन करने वाले राम, ब्रह्मा आदि देवताओं द्वारा प्रार्थित राम, सूर्यवंश के मण्डन दशरथनन्दन राम, कौसल्या का सुख बढ़ाने वाले राम, विश्वामित्र के प्रियधन राम, ताटका का वध करने वाले राम, शिवधनुष भंजक राम, सीता को वरमाला अर्पित करने वाले राम।

अयोध्याकाण्ड: अगणित गुणों से भूषित राम, पितृवाक्य से वन जाने वाले राम, चित्रकूट निवासी राम, भरत को अपनी पादुका देने वाले राम।

अरण्यकाण्ड: दण्डक वन को पावन करने वाले राम, शबरी के फल खाने वाले राम।

सुन्दरकाण्ड: सीता के प्राणाधार राम, हनुमान से सुशोभित राम।

युद्धकाण्ड: रावण वध के लिए प्रस्थित राम, दशमुख राक्षस का संहार करने वाले राम, पट्टाभिषेक से अलंकृत राम, समस्त लोकों का उद्धार करने वाले राम।

उत्तरकाण्ड: संसृति-बन्धन से मुक्त करने वाले, धर्मस्थापना में तत्पर, भक्तिपरायण को मुक्ति देने वाले, वैकुण्ठ में स्थित, नित्यानन्दपद में स्थित राम।

राम राम जय राजाराम! राम राम जय सीताराम!
benefits: Entire Ramayana condensed into divine names — ideal for daily recitation
Covers all 7 Kandas of Ramayana through Rama's names and epithets
Invokes the full merit of reading the complete ramayana
Extremely powerful nama-sankirtana for Rama devotees
Can be memorized and recited anywhere, anytime
Invokes the blessing of protection, dharma, and liberation through rama's grace
description: Dedicated to Lord Rama, the Nama Ramayanam (नाम रामायणम्) is a sacred prayer revered in Hindu tradition. Attributed to Traditional Vaishnavite Literature, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to cover all 7 Kandas of Ramayana through Rama's names and epithets and can be memorized and recited anywhere, anytime. It is also said to grant the full merit of reading the complete Ramayana. It is especially recommended on Thursday (Guruvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Ram Navami, Hanuman Jayanti, and Dussehra for maximum spiritual benefit. At 108 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 12 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Nama Ramayanam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Nama Ramayanam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. The complete Ramayana condensed into divine names of Lord Rama, covering all seven Kandas.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Nama Ramayanam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Nama Ramayanam (नाम रामायणम्) is a uniquely beautiful Sanskrit composition that narrates the entire Valmiki Ramayana through 108 sacred name-epithets (namani) of Lord Rama, organised under the seven traditional kandas (chapters) of the Ramayana. Each line ends with the divine refrain Rama, creating the rhythmic effect of continuous Rama-nama-sankirtana while simultaneously telling the complete story. The hymn opens with the supreme metaphysical declaration Suddha-Brahma-paratpara Rama ('Pure Brahman beyond the beyond, Rama') and proceeds chronologically: Bala-kanda names cover Rama's birth and Sita's wedding; Ayodhya-kanda names cover the exile; Aranya-kanda names cover forest events; Kishkindha and Sundara names cover the Hanuman-Sugriva episodes; Yuddha-kanda names cover the Lanka war; Uttara-kanda names culminate in Vaikuntha-alaya-samsthita Rama. It closes with the immortal refrain Rama Rama Jaya Raja Rama, Rama Rama Jaya Sita Rama. (Sources: traditional Vaishnavite Nama-Ramayana literature; Lakshmana Pandita's Sankshipta Ramayana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Nama Ramayanam organise Rama's names by kanda?
seoFaqs.answer: The 108 namani are organised across the seven kandas: (1) Bala-kanda (~22 names) — Suddha-Brahma, Kalatmaka, Sheshatalpa-sukha-nidrita, Dasharatha-nandana, Kausalya-sukha-vardhana, Vishvamitra-priya, Tataka-ghataka, Kaushika-makha-samrakshaka, Ahalya-uddharaka, Tryambaka-karmuka-bhanjaka, Sita-arpita-vara-malika; (2) Ayodhya-kanda (~12 names) — Pitri-vakya-shrita-kanana, Priya-Guha-vinivedita, Bharadvaja-mukha-anandaka, Chitrakuta-adri-niketana, Bharata-arpita-paduka; (3) Aranya-kanda (~14 names) — Dandaka-vana-pavana, Dushta-Viradha-vinashana, Agastya-anugraha, Khara-Dushana-mukha-sudaka, Shabari-datta-phala-ashana, Kabandha-bahu-chchhedaka; (4) Kishkindha (~5 names) — Hanumat-sevita-pada, Garvita-Vali-samharaka; (5) Sundara (~8 names) — Sita-prana-adharaka, Krita-chudamani-darshana; (6) Yuddha (~24 names) — Setu-bandhana, Kumbhakarna-shirah-chhedaka, Samhrita-Dashamukha, Pattabhisheka-alankrita; (7) Uttara (~20 names) — Sita-alingana-nirvrita, Ashvamedha-kratu-dikshita, Vaikuntha-alaya-samsthita, Nitya-nanda-pada-sthita. (Source: Nama Ramayanam structure.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Suddha-Brahma-paratpara Rama, the opening epithet?
seoFaqs.answer: Suddha-Brahma-paratpara Rama is the supreme metaphysical declaration that opens the Nama Ramayanam: Suddha — pure, unblemished, untouched by maya; Brahma — the supreme universal absolute (Brahman) of Vedanta; Paratpara — beyond the beyond, transcendent to even the transcendent (param + para — beyond the supreme); Rama — Rama. The total meaning: 'Rama is the pure Brahman beyond all transcendence.' This single opening epithet places the historical Rama (the prince of Ayodhya) firmly in the Vaishnava theological position as the Para-Brahman of the Upanishads — not merely an avatara but Vishnu Himself, indeed Brahman Himself. The next epithet Kalatmaka-Parameshvara Rama ('Rama, the Supreme Lord whose self is Time') and Shesha-talpa-sukha-nidrita Rama ('Rama, sleeping happily on the serpent Shesha-couch') equate Him with Maha-Vishnu reclining in Kshira-sagara. The hymn thus opens with cosmic Vedanta and then descends to the historical lila. (Source: Nama Ramayanam, opening verse; Vaishnava theological tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of the closing Rama Rama Jaya Raja Rama refrain?
seoFaqs.answer: The hymn closes with the celebrated refrain Rama Rama Jaya Raja Rama, Rama Rama Jaya Sita Rama — 'Rama Rama Victory to King Rama, Rama Rama Victory to Sita-Rama.' This is the classic Rama-nama-bhajan refrain sung in millions of Hindu households, temples, pilgrimages, satsangas and Ram-katha gatherings across the world for centuries. It is also the signature mantra of the Maharashtrian sant-tradition (Samartha Ramdas, Tukaram, Eknath) and the bhajan-mandali tradition. The refrain combines: (1) Rama Rama — the simple double-Rama-nama, of which Tulsidas writes Rama nama mani-deepa; (2) Jaya Raja Rama — victory salutation to Rama as Maharaja, the ideal king; (3) Jaya Sita Rama — the inseparable joint salutation to Sita-Rama, since Vaishnava theology never separates the divine couple. Reciting the refrain after the entire 108 names completes the Nama Ramayanam parayana with intense bhakti-rasa and seals the merit. (Sources: Nama Ramayanam; Maharashtrian Ramayan-bhajan tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the unique value of Nama Ramayanam compared to other Ramayanas?
seoFaqs.answer: The Nama Ramayanam offers benefits unmatched by other Ramayana versions: (1) entire Ramayana in only 108 names — the most condensed Ramayana possible, recitable in just 10-12 minutes; (2) every name ends with Rama — converting the recitation into continuous Rama-nama-sankirtana, which the Padma Purana, Brahma Vaivarta Purana and Kali Santarana Upanishad declare to be the supreme means of liberation in Kali Yuga; (3) the 108 names match the auspicious sacred number (108 namavalis, mala-beads, Upanishads, Lakshmi-stotras); (4) each name is itself a complete dhyana-padartha — a meditation-object on one specific aspect of Rama's lila; (5) being short, memorable and rhythmic, it can be chanted while walking, working, travelling or even silently in any free moment; (6) it carries the merit (phala) of full Valmiki Ramayana parayana (according to traditional belief); (7) it is suitable for children, elders, the unwell, the busy and even the linguistically untrained (since the names are short and rhythmic); (8) it is the most-recommended Ramayana for daily Kali-yuga sadhakas. (Sources: Nama Ramayanam tradition; Kali-Santarana Upanishad.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Nama Ramayanam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Nama Ramayanam is most powerfully recited daily at sunrise (Brahma-muhurta, 4-6 am) as the morning Rama-smaran, on Thursday (Guruvar — Vishnu/Rama day), Saturday (Shanivar — the day Hanuman pacified Shani for Rama's bhakta), every Ekadashi, Ram Navami (Chaitra Shukla Navami — Rama's birth, the supreme festival), Hanuman Jayanti (Chaitra Purnima), Sita Navami (Vaishakha Shukla Navami — Sita's birth), Vijayadashami / Dussehra (Ashvin Shukla Dashami — Ravana-vadha day), Akshaya Tritiya, Vaishakha Purnima, Vaikuntha Ekadashi (Margashirsha-Pausha Shukla Ekadashi), and during the 9-day Rama-Navaratri (Chaitra Shukla 1-9). Many devotees recite it 108 times per cycle on a tulsi or rudraksha mala, completing 108 full parayanas during a Rama-saptaha (week-long Ramayana parayana). It is also chanted during long pilgrimages to Ayodhya, Chitrakuta, Panchavati, Kishkindha, Rameshwaram, and along the Ramayan-yatra route. (Sources: Vaishnava Rama upasana paddhati; Drikpanchang Ram Navami observances.)
